source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 14.4.1 more dnf commands (pp. 263-267)

questions.q: List the packages currently installed on the system with dnf.
questions.why: dnf list --installed shows installed packages; plain 'dnf list' (=--all) lists everything available.

questions.q: Find which package provides the file /usr/bin/systemctl.
questions.why: dnf provides reports the package owning a given file (works even for not-installed packages, unlike rpm -qf).
dnf repolist shows enabled repos by default; --all lists all, --disabled only disabled ones.

questions.q: Download the tree package to the current directory without installing it.
questions.why: dnf download fetches the package locally; --downloaddir/--destdir choose where to save it.

questions.q: Clean all of dnf's cached data.
questions.why: dnf clean all removes all temporary/cached files for enabled repositories.

questions.q: Which dnf command adds, enables or disables repositories from the command line?
What is the difference between 'dnf list --updates' and 'dnf list --available'?
options: --updates shows packages you can upgrade; --available shows packages available to install
--updates shows installed packages; --available shows the cache
questions.why: --updates lists upgradable packages; --available lists installable ones (--installed lists what's on the system).
